reason: The phrase 'optimal quantum strategies' in the preamble is imprecise. The NPA hierarchy at finite level k provides RELAXATIONS of the set of quantum correlations, not the exact set. At finite k, feasible points include pseudo-correlations that are not realizable by any quantum strategy. So an 'extreme point of the level-k feasible set' is not necessarily an 'optimal quantum strategy' -- it may not correspond to any physical quantum state or strategy at all. The structural consequences (i)-(iii) apply to extreme points of the level-k SDP relaxation, which is a superset of the actual quantum correlations. The node should clarify that these are structural facts about the SDP relaxation, not directly about quantum strategies, unless k = infinity (convergence), in which case different issues arise as noted in the challenges to 1.10.1.
